name: refund
description: Refund orchestration. Answers 'has this payment been reversed, in full or in part?'. Ships TWO tables: `refunds` (root record — one per refund initiation; references payment + invoice + credit_note; carries reason + amount + provider result), `refund_lines` (per-invoice-line partial refunds — supports 'refund only the addon fee, not the membership'). Refunds are IMMUTABLE after processing — reversal via cancel path only when status=pending. Every refund creates: (1) offsetting Transaction via TransactionRecorder (debit refunds_owed, credit cash), (2) CreditNote via CreditNoteIssuer (reversal invoice), (3) provider-side refund call via PaymentProviderManager (returns provider reference + fee_reversal), (4) marketing::RefundIssued event (negative-value conversion — ad networks subtract from ROAS), (5) referrals::ClawbackReward when the original invoice had a referral reward materialized. Refund reasons: customer_requested / product_not_received / product_unacceptable / duplicate_charge / fraud_confirmed / subscription_cancelled / admin_correction / chargeback_prevention. Approval workflow — refunds > $X or issued > 30 days after payment require admin approval (workflow module integration). Full-refund vs partial-refund vs prorated-refund (for cancelled memberships — refund_amount computed from unused period + carried-over passes).
planned_consumers: chargeback (Wave 4 sibling — chargeback resolution may decide to issue a refund via this module rather than fight; the chargeback module dispatches RefundOrchestrator::create() with reason='chargeback_prevention')
growth::marketing (Wave 5 — every RefundSucceeded fires a marketing::RefundIssued negative-value conversion event; ad networks subtract from ROAS + retrain fraud models)
growth::referrals (Wave 5 — refund on an invoice with a materialized referral reward cascades a ClawbackReward event; the referrals module reverses the reward + notifies the referrer)

packages: owen-it/laravel-auditing ^13.x (compliance-grade audit trail — every write to refunds/refund_lines emits an audit row with 7-year retention; 10y Enterprise)
wraps.rationale: Provider-side refund dispatch flows through payment::PaymentProviderManager — this module does NOT own driver code. RefundProviderExecutor is a thin façade that resolves the correct provider driver from the original payment.provider + payment.provider_reference, then calls the driver's `createRefund(amount, reason)` method. Guzzle-level retry is disabled (owned by this module via CheckRefundProviderStatusJob) to preserve idempotency + circuit-breaker semantics inherited from payment module. laravel-auditing is the codebase-standard audit trait — every finance table uses it (see invoice + payment + transaction + tax modules).

tenant.description: Tenant-facing refund surface. Two audiences: customer routes (POST /refunds to request a refund on own payment, GET /refunds/mine, GET /refunds/{refund}, POST /refunds/{refund}/cancel while pending) and admin+finance routes (GET /refunds to list, POST /refunds to admin-initiate, POST /refunds/{refund}/approve, POST /refunds/{refund}/reject, POST /refunds/{refund}/force-fail, refund-line editor, POST /refunds/reconcile per provider, GET /refunds/pending-approvals, GET /reports/refund-volume). Every route carries `sanctum` guard + `X-Application-Id` + `entitlement.enforce:refund_capture`. Customer routes additionally carry `refund.enforce_customer_scope` middleware — a customer can only request refunds against payments where they were the customer.

platform-admin.description: Cross-tenant support tooling. Ops + support review refund status across tenants for escalation cases (stuck provider refunds, drift reconciliation), audit financial reversal records, and access refund evidence for compliance investigations. Redacted view — customer PII is hashed unless the caller holds platform.refund.pii.view.
